items: Video editor with `?timeline=` uses full viewport — no GlobalHeader, no SocialIcons footer, no `container mx-auto` constraint, no pane margins
Video editor WITHOUT `?timeline=` (timeline list) keeps normal layout with GlobalHeader and footer
Navigating away from /tools/video-editor restores normal layout completely
A minimal back bar (~32-40px) shows at top of editor with back link and timeline name
EditorPane dots are suppressed on the editor route
Generation items can be dragged from GenerationsPane and dropped onto the timeline to create clips, using the existing `application/x-generation` drag protocol
Generation items can be dragged onto the assets panel to register without creating a clip
Dropped assets include generationId and variantId in their AssetRegistryEntry
Dropped assets are persisted to the `asset_registry` DB column via registerAsset — survive page reload
Existing drag flows (asset-key from panel, file upload) continue working unchanged
Assets panel in PropertiesPanel is collapsed by default
Assets with generationId/variantId show a click-through action that opens MediaLightbox
No hardcoded color classes (zinc-*, gray-*, slate-*) remain in video editor components
Compact mode in EditorPane still works on non-editor routes
npm run build passes with no type errors

unresolved_flags.concern: The plan still does not identify a concrete repository-backed source for the primary variant ID/URL at drag time. In the current GenerationsPane data flow, gallery items are generation records, and the fields needed to drag the primary `generation_variants` row are not actually present on `image` today.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/shared/hooks/projects/useProjectGenerations.ts:287-304` selects generation fields from `generations` but does not select `primary_variant_id` or join `generation_variants`. `src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts:146-169` defines `RawGeneration` without `primary_variant_id`, and `src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts:263-375` transforms generations without adding any top-level `variantId`. `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/types.ts:18-62` also has no primary-variant field on `GeneratedImageWithMetadata`. The revised Step 4 says to read `image.metadata?.variant_id` or similar, but this repository path does not currently provide that data.

rationale: Score dropped from 10.0 to 0.5 — 5 of 6 flags resolved in one pass with an 82% plan delta. The remaining flag (variant ID not on generation records at drag time) is a real gap but is scoped to a single step (Step 4) and is discoverable during implementation. The plan already acknowledges this uncertainty in its questions and assumptions, noting it may need a query-cache lookup. This is the kind of detail best resolved by reading the actual data shape during execution rather than another planning iteration. The implementer can check what fields are available on the `image` prop and add a small query or cache lookup if `variant_id` isn't present — it won't change the plan's structure or other steps.
signals_assessment: Iteration 2. Weighted score trajectory: 10.0 → 0.5. Plan delta: 81.8%. No recurring critiques, no scope creep. 5 flags resolved, 1 remaining (variant ID sourcing). The remaining flag is correctness-category but implementation-scoped — it affects only the drag data enrichment in Step 4, not the overall architecture. Preflight checks all pass. The plan is structurally sound and ready for execution with a tactical note on the variant data source.
warnings: Step 4 must verify at implementation time what fields are actually available on the `image` prop in MediaGalleryItem. If `variant_id` is not present, the implementer should query the generation_variants table (or read from React Query cache) to get the primary variant row. This may add a small async lookup or require passing variant data through the gallery item props.
The plan assumes `image.url` in MediaGalleryItem IS the primary variant's URL — verify this is a direct storage URL and not a thumbnail or transformed URL.
orchestrator_guidance: Plan passed gate and preflight. Proceed to finalize. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.

signals.idea: Polish and integrate video editor into Reigh: full-screen editor page (hide GlobalHeader, use all vertical space), suppress EditorPane on editor route, drag from GenerationsPane into timeline/assets (variant-level), assets panel collapsed by default, generation variant click-through, aesthetic polish

unresolved_flags.concern: The plan still does not identify a concrete repository-backed source for the primary variant ID/URL at drag time. In the current GenerationsPane data flow, gallery items are generation records, and the fields needed to drag the primary `generation_variants` row are not actually present on `image` today.

resolved_flags.concern: The plan violates the required flat step structure. It uses phase headers with nested step headings instead of flat `## Step N:` sections.
resolved_flags.resolution: Addressed all 6 flags: (1) Flat `## Step N:` format throughout — no phase headers. (2) Full-screen gating now checks `searchParams.has('timeline')` in addition to pathname, so timeline list view keeps normal layout. (3) Identified and suppressed ALL layout constraints: `container mx-auto` wrapper in LayoutMainContent.tsx:64, `SocialIcons` footer in Layout.tsx:128-134, pane margins, GlobalProcessingWarning, containerPadding/containerSpacing. (4) Corrected drag source — `MediaGalleryItem.tsx:324` already has `draggable={!isMobile}` with `handleDragStart` calling `setGenerationDragData()` at line 267. Extended the existing `GenerationDropData` protocol instead of inventing a new one. (5) Variant data extracted from the generation's primary variant at drag time via existing `image` prop data, adding `variantId` and `variantType` to `GenerationDropData`. No query-level changes needed. (6) Added `registerAsset` method to `DataProvider`/`SupabaseDataProvider` that persists to the `asset_registry` column (extracted from existing `uploadAsset` logic). Drop handler calls both `patchRegistry` (local) and `registerAsset` (DB persist).

resolved_flags.concern: Pathname-only full-screen gating would also hide chrome for the timeline list view on `/tools/video-editor`, which conflicts with the plan's own assumption that only the active editor shell should get the full-screen treatment.

(3) Identified and suppressed ALL layout constraints: `container mx-auto` wrapper in LayoutMainContent.tsx:64, `SocialIcons` footer in Layout.tsx:128-134, pane margins, GlobalProcessingWarning, containerPadding/containerSpacing. (4) Corrected drag source — `MediaGalleryItem.tsx:324` already has `draggable={!isMobile}` with `handleDragStart` calling `setGenerationDragData()` at line 267. Extended the existing `GenerationDropData` protocol instead of inventing a new one. (5) Variant data extracted from the generation's primary variant at drag time via existing `image` prop data, adding `variantId` and `variantType` to `GenerationDropData`. No query-level changes needed. (6) Added `registerAsset` method to `DataProvider`/`SupabaseDataProvider` that persists to the `asset_registry` column (extracted from existing `uploadAsset` logic). Drop handler calls both `patchRegistry` (local) and `registerAsset` (DB persist).

resolved_flags.concern: The full-screen layout steps miss repo-level wrappers that still constrain the editor: the route outlet sits inside a centered `container mx-auto` shell, and the global social footer still renders below the page. Hiding the header and pane padding alone will not produce a true full-screen editor.

resolved_flags.concern: The drag plan targets the wrong source component and introduces a parallel drag protocol. Gallery drag starts in `MediaGalleryItem`, not `DraggableImage`, and existing gallery-to-shot drops already use the shared generation drag helpers.

resolved_flags.concern: The proposed 'register asset' drop flow is not compatible with the current persistence model. `patchRegistry` only mutates local editor state; it does not persist `asset_registry`, and `saveTimeline` writes only timeline config.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 2. Weighted score trajectory: 10.0 -> 0.5. Plan deltas: 81.8%. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 5. Open significant flags: 1.
